review_metadata.reviewer_focus: Launch readiness, UI visibility, monetization, performance, bug audit
review_metadata.overall_status: 75-80% ready - critical issues found in UI rendering and paywall logic
review_metadata.recommendation: Fix critical bugs before public launch; resolve floating bubbles visibility issue and paywall verification flow

floating_bubbles_not_visible.issue: User reported floating bubbles/petals background is not visible despite code existing in components.tsx
root_causes: Petals have very low opacity (0.08 light, 0.12 dark) - intended subtle effect but might be TOO subtle
Animated.View transforms use parseFloat on interpolated values; platform compatibility issue possible
nativeDriver = Platform.OS !== 'web' - Android/iOS might not render animations correctly
Petals positioned absolutely at percentages; could be clipped by View overflow or z-index layering
useNativeDriver=true for sine easing might not support complex animations on all platforms
floating_bubbles_not_visible.evidence: File: src/shared/components.tsx lines 66-120; opacity set to 0.08-0.12, Animated.loop with Easing.inOut(Easing.sin)
recommended_fixes: Increase petal opacity: 0.15-0.25 (light), 0.20-0.30 (dark) for subtle but visible effect
Test on actual iOS/Android devices via Expo Go - web might work but mobile might not
Add console.log() to debug: track if petals array is populated, animation starts
Verify overflow:hidden on atmosphere View isn't clipping petals outside viewport
Consider disabling useNativeDriver for web platform specifically
Add blur or backdrop filter fallback for browsers that don't support rounded shapes well

paywall_verification_incomplete.issue: Production payment verification not implemented; sandbox mode forces all real payments to fail
paywall_verification_incomplete.details: verifyPayment() in payment.ts only accepts SANDBOX* transaction IDs in non-production; real Khalti/eSewa integration is missing
paywall_verification_incomplete.code: if (!cleanId.toUpperCase().startsWith('SANDBOX')) { return { ok: false, state: 'failed', message: 'Sandbox mode only accepts...' }; }
paywall_verification_incomplete.impact: Users cannot complete actual purchases. Premium tier activation will fail in production.
paywall_verification_incomplete.recommended_action: Implement production verification backend or partner with payment provider's client SDK

habit_score_calculation_flawed.issue: Habit score parts can exceed their maximums due to bonus/penalty logic
habit_score_calculation_flawed.details: Lifestyle score: base 20, can lose up to 17 points penalties + 4 reward points = potential negative or >20 value before Math.max/Math.min
habit_score_calculation_flawed.code_snippet: const lifestylePenalty = (stressHigh?3:0) + (noMovement?3:0) + ... (up to 17 total); const lifestyle = Math.max(0, Math.min(20, lifestyleBase - lifestylePenalty + lifestyleReward));
habit_score_calculation_flawed.actual_issue: If multiple penalties apply, lifestyle score could be 0 even with rewards. Need better weighting.
habit_score_calculation_flawed.fix_priority: MEDIUM - works but unintuitive for users
habit_score_calculation_flawed.recommended_fix: Refactor lifestyle scoring to use percentages or weighted system; ensure parts always total 100

pros: Comprehensive 8-step flow covers profile, symptoms, lifestyle, environment, routine, and consent
Bilingual (EN/NE) with clear field instructions
Default fallback profile prevents crashes if user skips
Quiz data properly stored in AppContext and persisted to AsyncStorage
cons: No progress indicator (step 1/8) - users don't know how far they are
No 'go back' button - one-way flow could frustrate users
Missing validation on multi-select (symptoms, primaryConcerns) - users might accidentally submit empty
Selfie capture is optional but not clearly marked as optional
No loading state if image compression takes time
improvements: Add step indicator (Step 1 of 8, Step 2 of 8, etc.)
Show required vs optional field badges
Add validation error toast if primary concerns are empty
onboarding_quiz.launch_readiness: GO - works as-is but UX could be polished

pros: Rule-based matching system is transparent and auditable (not a black-box AI)
Handles multiple conditions correctly, sorts by score, limits to top 3
Merges duplicate steps intelligently (e.g., two cleansers → one best cleanser)
Nepal-specific context tips and water quality guidance are well-implemented
Fallback routines prevent crashes if no match found
cons: Knowledge base is hardcoded JSON; no CMS or easy update path for new conditions
Scoring logic is complex and hard to debug; 14 different scoring factors
No A/B testing framework to validate which scoring weights work best
Weekly routines might be too ambitious (6 steps) for some users
routine_generation_engine.performance: FAST - JSON matching completes in <50ms even with complex profiles

pros: Clear breakdown of 6 score components visible to users
Reasons explain why score is lower/higher (transparency builds trust)
Captures real skincare behavior (routine, care, lifestyle, weather, wellness, logs)
Animated progress bar provides visual feedback
cons: Score calculation has edge cases: if all penalties apply, lifestyle = 0 regardless of other factors
Weather readiness scoring relies on external environment data (AQI, UV) that might fail
Selfie logging is optional but heavily weighted (2 points out of 5 for logs)
No historical trending - score shown daily but not compared to 7-day average
Users can game the score by marking weather actions as 'prepared' without actually doing them

bugs.description: Lifestyle penalties can sum to >20, causing score to be 0 when multiple issues exist
bugs.impact: Users with high stress + no exercise + high junk food all at once get 0 lifestyle score even if trying
bugs.fix: Normalize penalties or use percentage-based scoring

habit_score_system.launch_readiness: CAUTION - scores seem low but users may not understand why

pros: Weekly Skin Report structure is well-designed (best habit, trigger, why changed, next week focus)
Habit score breakdown is clear and actionable
Daily check-in captures comprehensive lifestyle data (water, sleep, stress, screen time, movement)
Selfie upload is implemented with image picker
cons: Weekly report is LOCKED behind premium paywall even though 50% of the data is free
No photo timeline visualization despite `buildWeeklySkinReport()` existing
7-day plan suggestions are generic - not personalized to user's actual weaknesses
No export or PDF generation - users can't share progress with dermatologists
Check-in data not synced to Firebase (local-only), so progress lost if app uninstalled
feature_gaps: Missing: Sharing progress reports with doctors
progress_tracking_and_reporting.launch_readiness: PARTIAL - core tracking works but reporting is half-baked

pros: 112 pre-curated products with Nepal-specific sourcing (Daraz, local pharmacies)
Trust score, fake-product risk warnings, and ingredient breakdowns provided
Budget-tier filtering working correctly (under200, 200-500, 500+)
Sort options (recommended, price, trust, ads last) are intuitive
Affiliate links point to Daraz for revenue generation
cons: Free tier limited to only 6 products per category - too restrictive
Sponsored/Ad labels visible but no clear disclosure that these are affiliate picks
Fake-product risk shown but no explanation of HOW to spot fake (e.g., packaging check, seller rating)
No product reviews from real users - all pre-written
Budget alternatives shown only to premium users; free users can't see cheaper options
No 'add to shopping list' or save for later feature
product_recommendations.monetization: WORKING - paywall is properly gating deeper product insights
product_recommendations.launch_readiness: GO - but consider loosening free tier limit to 10 products

pros: Micro-tips are contextual and personalized (e.g., acne → hands off, spot treat)
Like/Save actions working with proper state persistence
Ingredient checker is functional and educational
Skin modes (event, hostel, winter, etc.) are creative and well-designed
Bilingual content provided
cons: Tip feed is static - no new tips added over time (not a live feed)
Favorite tips card flickers on every render (missing useMemo)
Budget routine builder premium feature should be free (helps free users stay engaged)
Skin mode previews are text-only; no visual representation
No share-to-WhatsApp/social for tips despite having share button

pros: Q&A structure is clear and searchable
Bilingual answers provided
Clinic escalation warning shows up appropriately
Premium ask-an-expert feature future-proofed
cons: Q&A is completely static - 50+ pre-written answers, no user submission
Premium users cannot actually ask questions - feature not fully implemented
No expert validation system or badges (mentioned in code but not visible)
No search/filter on Q&As; users must scroll through all 50+
Misleading UI: 'Ask-an-expert' button for free users just shows paywall, no actual Q&A submission flow
community_qna.feature_completeness: 30% - only read-only Q&As work, ask feature not implemented
community_qna.launch_readiness: PARTIAL - works as read-only but missing ask feature

pros: Article structure with sections, takeaways, glossary terms, and nutrients
Reading time estimator helps users choose
Glossary terms defined inline (e.g., melasma explanation)
'When to see doctor' section provides safety guidance
Bilingual article support
cons: Premium paywall on full articles even though summaries are often enough
No search across all articles - only browse by category
No 'bookmark' or 'save for later' feature in free tier
Article content is static; no scheduled new articles
No related articles suggestions

pros: Paywall screen lists clear premium features with good marketing copy
Billing options (monthly, yearly, beta) are well-presented
UI/UX for paywall is polished
cons: CRITICAL: Payment verification does NOT work - sandbox-only mode means real purchases fail
Tier switching logic seems to work but is not persisted to Firebase
Free → Premium upgrade triggers `setTier('premium')` but subscription state might not sync
No receipt validation or expiry checking
No in-app restore purchase flow for users who already paid
Paywall can be bypassed by manually setting tier in state (security risk)
launch_blockers: No subscription expiry checking (users could stay premium forever if bug in state)
premium_paywall.launch_readiness: CRITICAL FIX NEEDED - DO NOT LAUNCH until Khalti/eSewa integration is complete

strengths: Consistent color palette (blush, sage, turmeric) feels cohesive and feminine
Card-based layout is modern and mobile-friendly
Light/dark mode properly implemented with smooth transitions
Micro-interactions (hover states, button press animations) feel premium
issues: CRITICAL: Floating bubbles/petals background NOT VISIBLE (see critical_findings section)
Tab bar height (64px) is large; reduces screen real estate on mobile
Some text colors don't have enough contrast in light mode (muted text)
Animated components might cause stuttering on lower-end Android devices

pros: ScrollView-based layouts handle different screen heights
Card padding scales appropriately (md = 16px)
Tab navigation is touch-friendly (icons + labels)
Text sizes are readable (H1, H2, Body, muted clearly distinguished)
cons: No testing evidence on actual iPhone SE or Android Mid-range devices
Landscape orientation not tested
No tablet layout (iPad experience not optimized)
Floating action buttons (theme/language toggle) might be in the way on small screens
mobile_responsiveness.recommendation: Test on actual devices before launch

issues: No accessible labels on Pressable components (missing accessible role)
Color-only information in progress bars (should have text labels)
Animated elements might cause motion sickness for users with vestibular disorders
Icon-only buttons (theme toggle) have no labels
improvements: Add accessibilityLabel to all interactive elements
Add reduceMotionEnabled check to disable animations for users who prefer reduced motion
Ensure color contrast meets WCAG AA standards

optimization_opportunities: Lazy-load article content (currently all loaded at startup)
Compress product catalog (112 products = ~50KB of JSON)
Remove unused Expo plugins if possible

runtime_performance.state_updates: GOOD - useMemo used correctly in Progress, Home, Tips screens
potential_issues: AppContext re-renders entire app when profile updates - could be optimized with reducer pattern
AsyncStorage writes on every state change - might cause jank on lower-end devices
Image loading in progress screen (selfie) not optimized for slow networks

database_sync.firebase_status: Service layer exists but not fully integrated; sync is manual, not real-time
issues: User data lost if app uninstalled (no cloud backup)
Progress photos stored locally only (not scalable)

pros: Consent flow exists (consentAccepted flag)
Legal/Privacy screens present
No sensitive data logged to console
concerns: User profile stored unencrypted in AsyncStorage (local device storage)
Payment provider credentials not visible but need verification that they're in backend only
No SSL pinning for API calls
Data export JSON contains all user data - no PII redaction before export
Firebase security rules not visible in codebase

issues: No user authentication means no multi-device sync
No user account recovery if data is lost
No way to track which user made purchases (for payment reconciliation)

payment_security.recommendation: Use official Khalti/eSewa SDKs; never roll custom payment validation

bugs_and_edge_cases.title: Floating petals not visible
bugs_and_edge_cases.description: Animated petals in AppAtmosphere component not rendering or too faint
bugs_and_edge_cases.steps_to_reproduce: Open app in light or dark mode on web or mobile
bugs_and_edge_cases.expected: Subtle animated petal shapes floating in background

bugs_and_edge_cases.title: Payment verification fails on production
bugs_and_edge_cases.description: verifyPayment() only accepts SANDBOX* transaction IDs; real payments fail
bugs_and_edge_cases.impact: Premium tier cannot be activated with real money

bugs_and_edge_cases.title: Lifestyle habit score can be negative
bugs_and_edge_cases.description: Multiple penalties can exceed base 20 score, resulting in 0 lifestyle score even with high effort
bugs_and_edge_cases.impact: Users confused why lifestyle score is 0 despite trying

bugs_and_edge_cases.title: No validation on required quiz fields
bugs_and_edge_cases.description: Users can submit quiz with empty symptoms or primary concerns
bugs_and_edge_cases.expected: Show error and require at least one symptom
bugs_and_edge_cases.actual: Silently accepts empty and generates fallback routine
bugs_and_edge_cases.impact: Users might skip important fields thinking they're optional

bugs_and_edge_cases.title: Tier state not persisted to Firebase
bugs_and_edge_cases.description: When user upgrades to premium, tier is updated locally but not synced to Firebase
bugs_and_edge_cases.impact: Premium status lost on reinstall or multi-device login

bugs_and_edge_cases.title: Weather actions data can fail silently
bugs_and_edge_cases.description: If environment.ts fails to fetch AQI/UV data, habit score uses fallback without warning user
bugs_and_edge_cases.impact: Habit score might be inaccurate on days with network issues
bugs_and_edge_cases.fix_priority: NICE TO HAVE - show network error message

current_model.free_tier: Core routine, daily tips, basic product recommendations, Q&A (limited), learn guides (basic)
current_model.premium_tier: Advanced insights, full product details, personalized skin modes, full Q&A archive

issues: CRITICAL: Payment verification not working - zero revenue until fixed
Free tier might be too limited - only 6 products shown vs. 112 available
Paywall shown in 6+ places; might feel aggressive to new users
No freemium mechanics to drive conversion (e.g., 'upgrade to see your full report')
Affiliate revenue model not validated - unclear if Daraz will approve volume
opportunities: Personalized skincare product bundles (morning + evening routine bundles)
Premium content: exclusive videos from dermatologists
Annual budget routine recommendations (seasonal skincare shopping guide)
monetization_analysis.recommendation: Fix payment verification FIRST. Then A/B test paywall placement to optimize conversion rate.

high_impact_low_effort.title: Add step indicator to onboarding quiz

high_impact_low_effort.title: Add validation error when quiz symptoms are empty

high_impact_low_effort.title: Show progress 7-day trend graph

high_impact_low_effort.title: Add 'share progress report' button

medium_impact_medium_effort.effort: 4-6 hours - build Firebase backend or use Contentful

medium_impact_medium_effort.title: Implement ask-an-expert feature for premium
medium_impact_medium_effort.effort: 3-4 hours - build Q&A submission + moderation queue

deferred_features: AR skin analysis camera (complex, low user demand)
AI-powered routine optimization (not needed - rule-based is more explainable)
Real-time community posts (requires moderation, scale challenges)

CRITICAL_FIX_BEFORE_LAUNCH.action: Implement Khalti or eSewa production API integration; use official SDK
Increase petal opacity to 0.18-0.25; test on iOS/Android devices
Implement Firebase Auth (email/phone); sync data to user account

HIGH_PRIORITY_BEFORE_PUBLIC_BETA.action: Add step counter + validation + back button

HIGH_PRIORITY_BEFORE_PUBLIC_BETA.issue: Habit score logic flawed (lifestyle can be 0)

Add trend chart to progress screen using react-native-chart-kit

MEDIUM_PRIORITY_NEXT_2_WEEKS.issue: Free tier too restrictive (6 products only)

Implement Firestore offline persistence + sync queue

must_haves.item: Payment verification working with real Khalti/eSewa
Floating petals visible on mobile and web
tested on real iOS (iPhone SE) and Android (Pixel 4)

show_stoppers: Floating petals not visible (HIGH - affects design integrity)

timeline_breakdown.day_1: Fix payment verification (2-3 hours), increase petal opacity (30 mins), add onboarding validation (1 hour)
timeline_breakdown.day_2: Implement Firebase Auth (3-4 hours), test on devices
timeline_breakdown.day_3_4: Refactor habit score logic, add progress trending, testing & bug fixes
timeline_breakdown.day_5: QA on iPhone + Android, final polish, app store submission

conclusion.overall_assessment: Prabha Skincare has solid fundamentals but is NOT READY for public launch. Core features (routine generation, progress tracking, product recs) are well-built and functional. However, three critical issues prevent launch: (1) payment verification is not implemented, (2) user authentication is missing, and (3) floating UI elements are not visible.
strengths: Clean, modern design with consistent Nepali-first approach
Rule-based skincare logic is transparent and auditable
Comprehensive feature set for MVP (onboarding, routine, progress, products, tips, Q&A, learn)
weaknesses: Payment processing is incomplete and non-functional
No user authentication or multi-device sync
Floating bubble visual effect (flagship design) is invisible
Knowledge base is static (no easy content updates)
conclusion.recommendation_to_founder: Focus on fixing the 3 show-stoppers FIRST (payment, auth, UI). You have 3-5 days of work left. Then do final QA on real devices. The core app is good—these are fixable issues, not architectural flaws. Launch on week of May 20 is feasible if team prioritizes ruthlessly.
next_steps: 1. IMMEDIATE: Implement Khalti API integration (or partner for backend verification)
2. IMMEDIATE: Increase petal opacity from 0.08 → 0.20; test on iOS/Android
3. URGENT: Add Firebase Auth (email signup); sync user data to accounts
4. Add onboarding validation + step indicators (easy UX win)
6. Test on real iPhone SE + Android device
7. Submit to App Store + Google Play (1-3 day review)
